page.content: In the analysis presented in Table 1, the variable Age at diagnosis shows a univariate relative risk (RR) of 4.012 with a 95% confidence interval (CI) of 1.895 to 8.493 and a P value of < 0.001, while the multivariate RR is 2.175 with a 95% CI of 0.607 to 7.792 and a P value of 0.233. Clinical stage reveals an univariate RR of 10.686 (95% CI: 3.842 – 29.719, P < 0.001) and a multivariate RR of 2.376 (95% CI: 0.527 – 10.716, P = 0.260). MYCN status presents an univariate RR of 3.723 (95% CI: 2.195 – 6.315, P < 0.001) and a multivariate RR of 2.191 (95% CI: 1.120 – 4.286, P = 0.022). MDA5 expression indicates an univariate RR of 4.718 (95% CI: 2.080 – 10.705, P < 0.001) and a multivariate RR of 2.811 (95% CI: 1.127 – 7.013, P = 0.027). Histology shows an univariate RR of 3.604 (95% CI: 2.037 – 6.376, P < 0.001) and a multivariate RR of 1.353 (95% CI: 0.680 – 2.695, P = 0.389). Table 2 details analysis of variance with sources including Line (d.f.
